section.heading: Surveys for, location, and construction of irrigation works generally

content.p: The Secretary of the Interior is authorized and directed to make examinations and surveys for, and to locate and construct, as herein provided, irrigation works for the storage, diversion, and development of waters, including artesian wells.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Herein, referred to in text, means in act June 17, 1902, popularly known as the Reclamation Act, which is classified generally to this chapter. For complete classification of act June 17, 1902, to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 371 of this title and Tables.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: Provisions similar to those in this section were contained in acts Mar. 2, 1889, ch. 411, § 1, 25 Stat. 960; Oct. 2, 1888, ch. 1069, § 1, 25 Stat. 526.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1946—Act Aug. 7, 1946, struck out provisions requiring annual reports to Congress as to results of those examinations and surveys.

note.heading: Section as Unaffected by Submerged Lands Act
note.p: Provisions of this section as not amended, modified or repealed by the Submerged Lands Act, see section 1303 of this title.
